message: We just closed on a 12-unit multifamily in Columbus. It took 18 months of relationship building, 27 property tours, and 3 failed offers to get here. The pro forma projects a 7% cash-on-cash return in year 1, with conservative rents. I put in $50,000 and partnered for the 20% down. Here are 5 lessons I wish I knew earlier.

Built 3 businesses over 10 years. The first two stalled; the 3rd scaled after I tracked weekly cash flow, delegated, and said 'no' to low-ROI gigs. Revenue crossed $1,200,000 last year with 22% margins. If you're early, focus on systems, not hacks. More soon.

People ask how I think about diversification. In 2016 I was 95% in my main company. Now it's 40% operating companies, 35% real estate, 15% index funds, 10% cash. Every quarter I review allocation, rebalance within plus/minus 5%, and keep dry powder for 2 deals per year.

This week I mentored a founder who grew from $0 to $250,000 ARR in 9 months but felt stuck. We mapped bottlenecks: 1) pricing, 2) churn, 3) founder time. A simple pricing test lifted ARPU by 12% and freed 6 hours/week. Small changes compound.
